steps.title: Use a modifying phrase
steps.content: The subject is "Wisconsin," and the main verb is "reaches." The words set off by commas between them must be a modifier, not another finite verb, or the sentence would have conflicting verb structure.

steps.title: Choose the participle form
steps.content: A and C are finite present-tense verbs, which would create an ungrammatical interruption between subject and main verb. D does not fit the active descriptive meaning. The present participle forms a phrase modifying Wisconsin. The answer is B.
